title: Timesheet Management with Two-Stage Approval Workflow + Published Schedule Visibility

requirements.text: Add a `Timesheet` data type and stable map to `backend/main.mo`. A Timesheet record must include: id (Text), employeeId (Text), employeeUsername (Text), entries (array of { date: Text, startTime: Text, endTime: Text, hoursWorked: Float }), totalHours (Float), status (variant: #submitted | #managerApproved | #managerDenied | #hrApproved | #hrDenied), submittedAt (Int nanoseconds), managerComment (optional Text), hrComment (optional Text), weekStartDate (Text). Add backend endpoints: `submitTimesheet` (employee only — creates a new timesheet record with status #submitted), `getTimesheets` (returns all timesheets; HR Admin sees all, Manager sees their team's, Employee sees only their own), `managerReviewTimesheet` (Manager or HR Admin — approves or denies a #submitted timesheet, sets status to #managerApproved or #managerDenied, stores managerComment), `hrReviewTimesheet` (HR Admin only — approves or denies a #managerApproved timesheet, sets status to #hrApproved or #hrDenied, stores hrComment). Only HR Admin and Manager callers may call the review endpoints. Return `#ok` or `#err` with descriptive messages.

quotes: add a timesheet management tab on HR and manager tab and a timesheet tab on employee

employees can enter there times into the timesheet the days they worked and when they hit submit it sends it to manager to approve it or deny ot when they approve or deny it send it to HR for final approval or denial

acceptanceCriteria: Timesheet record type is defined with all required fields in main.mo
`submitTimesheet` creates a record with status #submitted and is callable only by employee-role callers
`getTimesheets` returns all timesheets for HR Admin, team timesheets for Manager, own timesheets for Employee
`managerReviewTimesheet` transitions #submitted → #managerApproved or #managerDenied
`hrReviewTimesheet` transitions #managerApproved → #hrApproved or #hrDenied
Unauthorized callers receive a #err response

requirements.text: In `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`, add React Query hooks for the timesheet feature: `useTimesheets` (query — fetches all timesheets visible to the current user), `useSubmitTimesheet` (mutation — calls `submitTimesheet` backend endpoint, maps #ok/#err to resolved/rejected), `useManagerReviewTimesheet` (mutation — calls `managerReviewTimesheet`, maps #ok/#err), `useHrReviewTimesheet` (mutation — calls `hrReviewTimesheet`, maps #ok/#err). On success of any mutation, invalidate the timesheets query cache.

quotes: add a timesheet management tab on HR and manager tab and a timesheet tab on employee

acceptanceCriteria: `useTimesheets` fetches data and returns loading/error/data states
`useSubmitTimesheet` resolves on #ok and rejects on #err
`useManagerReviewTimesheet` and `useHrReviewTimesheet` resolve on #ok and reject on #err
All mutations invalidate the timesheets query cache on success

requirements.text: Create `frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page allows employees to build and submit a weekly timesheet. The UI must include: a week selector (defaults to current week), a table with one row per day of the selected week (Monday–Sunday) showing date, start time input, end time input, and auto-calculated hours, a running total hours display, a 'Submit Timesheet' button that calls `useSubmitTimesheet`, inline validation errors if start/end times are missing for any filled row, a toast notification on successful submission, and a section below the form listing the employee's previously submitted timesheets with their status badges (#submitted = 'Pending Manager Review', #managerApproved = 'Pending HR Review', #managerDenied = 'Denied by Manager', #hrApproved = 'Approved', #hrDenied = 'Denied by HR'). Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

quotes: a timesheet tab on employee employees can enter there times into the timesheet the days they worked and when they hit submit it sends it to manager to approve it or deny ot

acceptanceCriteria: Page renders a weekly timesheet entry grid with start/end time inputs per day
Hours auto-calculate per row and total is shown
Submit calls useSubmitTimesheet and shows a success toast
Validation blocks submission if any active row is missing start or end time
Previously submitted timesheets are listed with correct status badges
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

requirements.text: Create `frontend/src/pages/manager/ManagerTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page shows the manager a list of all timesheets submitted by their team members, split into two sections: 'Pending Your Review' (status = #submitted) and 'Reviewed / Processed' (all other statuses). For each pending timesheet, show employee name, week date range, total hours, and Approve/Deny action buttons. Clicking Approve calls `useManagerReviewTimesheet` with approved=true and an optional comment. Clicking Deny opens a small inline modal requiring a denial comment, then calls `useManagerReviewTimesheet` with approved=false and the comment. Show a toast on success or error. Reviewed timesheets show their current status badge and any comments. Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

quotes: it sends it to manager to approve it or deny ot when they approve or deny it send it to HR for final approval or denial

acceptanceCriteria: Pending timesheets (status #submitted) appear in the 'Pending Your Review' section
Approve action calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=true
Deny action requires a comment and calls useManagerReviewTimesheet with approved=false
Success toast appears on approval or denial
Reviewed timesheets appear in the second section with status badges and comments
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

requirements.text: Create `frontend/src/pages/hr/HRTimesheetPage.tsx`. This page shows HR Admin all timesheets that have been manager-approved (status = #managerApproved) in a 'Pending Final HR Approval' section, and all other processed timesheets (all statuses) in an 'All Timesheets' section with filtering by status and employee name search. For each manager-approved timesheet, show employee name, week date range, total hours, manager comment, and Final Approve/Final Deny action buttons. Clicking Final Approve calls `useHrReviewTimesheet` with approved=true. Clicking Final Deny opens an inline modal requiring an HR comment, then calls `useHrReviewTimesheet` with approved=false. Show a toast on success or error. Match the dark navy/amber visual theme.

quotes: when they approve or deny it send it to HR for final approval or denial

acceptanceCriteria: Manager-approved timesheets (status #managerApproved) appear in the 'Pending Final HR Approval' section
Final Approve calls useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=true
Final Deny requires an HR comment and calls useHrReviewTimesheet with approved=false
Success toast appears on final approval or denial
All Timesheets section shows all records with search and status filter
Page matches the existing dark navy/amber visual theme

requirements.text: Add a 'Timesheets' navigation link to the HR Admin sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/HRAdminLayout.tsx` pointing to `/hr/timesheets`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the HR Admin route tree, mapping `/hr/timesheets` to `HRTimesheetPage`. Add a 'Timesheets' navigation link to the Manager sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/ManagerLayout.tsx` pointing to `/manager/timesheets`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the Manager route tree, mapping `/manager/timesheets` to `ManagerTimesheetPage`. Add a 'Timesheet' navigation link to the Employee sidebar in `frontend/src/layouts/EmployeeLayout.tsx` pointing to `/employee/timesheet`. Register the route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` under the Employee route tree, mapping `/employee/timesheet` to `EmployeeTimesheetPage`.

quotes: add a timesheet management tab on HR and manager tab and a timesheet tab on employee

acceptanceCriteria: HR Admin sidebar shows a 'Timesheets' link that navigates to /hr/timesheets and renders HRTimesheetPage
Manager sidebar shows a 'Timesheets' link that navigates to /manager/timesheets and renders ManagerTimesheetPage
Employee sidebar shows a 'Timesheet' link that navigates to /employee/timesheet and renders EmployeeTimesheetPage
All three routes are protected by their respective role guards

requirements.text: Ensure that when a schedule is published (shift status transitions from #draft to #published via `publishSchedule`), the employee's schedule page (`frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeSchedulePage.tsx`) and employee dashboard (`frontend/src/pages/employee/EmployeeDashboard.tsx`) immediately reflect the newly published shifts without requiring a page reload. The `useShifts` or equivalent query used in those pages must refetch published shifts automatically — either via a short polling interval (e.g., every 30 seconds) or by ensuring the React Query stale time is set to 0 so the data is always considered stale and refetched on mount. Published shifts must be visible to employees the moment they navigate to their schedule page after a publish action.

quotes: Make it where when staff schedules are publish that staff can view that schedule instantly and see when they work

acceptanceCriteria: EmployeeSchedulePage.tsx fetches only published shifts (status #published)
The shifts query has staleTime: 0 or refetchInterval set so data is always fresh on mount
After a manager or HR Admin publishes a schedule, an employee navigating to their schedule page sees the new shifts
Draft shifts (status #draft) are NOT visible to employees on the schedule page or dashboard
EmployeeDashboard.tsx upcoming shifts section also only shows published shifts

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture)
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
The two-stage approval workflow must be enforced: Manager reviews first (#submitted → #managerApproved/#managerDenied), then HR Admin reviews (#managerApproved → #hrApproved/#hrDenied)
HR Admin cannot skip the manager stage — hrReviewTimesheet must only act on #managerApproved records
Employee-facing schedule pages must only show shifts with status #published
nonGoals: Timesheet payroll calculation or integration with external payroll systems
Email or push notification delivery when timesheet status changes
Supervisor role access to timesheets (not requested)
Editing or deleting submitted timesheets after submission
Automatic timesheet generation from shift data
